note: Researched seed spots for the Columbia Valley / Golden area. Ratings are reputation-based estimates - adjust after fishing. Verify current regs in the BC Region 4 (Kootenay) synopsis.

spots.name: Whitetail Lake
spots.detail: Trophy lake up Findlay Creek / Whitetail Lake Rd, NW of Canal Flats
spots.season: Ice-off (May) and fall; chironomids spring, leeches/boatmen fall
spots.access: Gravel forest road from Findlay Creek FSR. Managed as a TROPHY lake - special quota/size restrictions apply, check Region 4 synopsis before keeping anything.
spots.notes: The trophy stillwater of the Columbia Valley - stocked with Gerrard-strain rainbows that grow big. Fishing is technical, not fast, but the size makes up for it. Seeded ratings are reputation-based - adjust after you fish it.

spots.name: Whiteswan Lake
spots.detail: Main lake in Whiteswan Lake Provincial Park, east of Canal Flats
spots.season: May-Sept; spawners visible in inlet/outlet creeks May-June; ice fishing in winter
spots.access: Hwy 93/95, turn east 4.5 km south of Canal Flats onto Whiteswan FSR, ~25 km gravel - active logging road, drive with caution. Two boat launches, campgrounds in the park. Lussier Hot Springs on the way in.
spots.notes: One of the highest-quality rainbow fisheries in the East Kootenay; self-sustaining population (stocking ended 2009), fish to 21"+. Very popular - go midweek if you can. Seeded ratings are reputation-based.

spots.name: Alces Lake
spots.detail: Small lake beside Whiteswan, same park - fly fishing only
spots.access: Same Whiteswan FSR access, first lake you reach. FLY FISHING ONLY, no power boats, no towing. Campground on the lake.
spots.notes: The quieter, prettier sister to Whiteswan - quality rainbows, moose in the shallows at dawn. Ideal if you fly fish. Seeded ratings are reputation-based.

spots.name: Premier Lake
spots.detail: Premier Lake Provincial Park, east of Skookumchuck (~1 hr south of Fairmont)
spots.season: Late April-July best; chironomids on 20-25 ft leaders early season
spots.access: Hwy 93/95 to 12 km north of Skookumchuck, marked exit east ~12 km to the park. Concrete boat launch, 57 campsites. Regs: rainbow 2/day; south end closed May 15-June 20 (spawning); no towing south half.
spots.notes: The most consistent producer in the area - heavily stocked (55,000+ rainbows/yr) and the source of Blackwater broodstock for the Kootenay hatchery. Great first stop while you learn the valley. Seeded ratings are reputation-based.

spots.name: Toby Creek (lower)
spots.detail: Panorama down to the Columbia confluence near Athalmer - fish the lower canyon runs and pockets
spots.season: July-Oct once runoff clears; early mornings good
spots.access: Follows Toby Creek Rd toward Panorama - lots of roadside pull-offs, easy wading access. Bull trout must be released in streams; check Region 4 stream regs.
spots.notes: Invermere's backyard creek - convenient and scenic more than trophy. Locals say Horsethief and Toby are decent but the famous cutthroat water is further south (St. Mary). My line traces the road corridor - redraw on satellite to mark the exact runs you like. Seeded ratings are reputation-based.

spots.name: Columbia River & Wetlands at Golden
spots.detail: Nicholson up to the Kicking Horse confluence - fish the tributary creek mouths
spots.season: Late summer-fall when water drops and clears
spots.access: Boat/canoe launches at Nicholson and Golden. The wetlands are the largest intact wetlands in North America - slow water, best explored by canoe. Columbia mainstem has specific regs - check synopsis.
spots.notes: The main river is silty and slow through here - the fishing is at creek mouths (bull trout ambush points) rather than the mainstem itself. As much a wildlife paddle as a fishery. My line is rough - redraw as you learn it. Seeded ratings are reputation-based.

spots.name: Blaeberry River (lower)
spots.detail: Lower valley NE of Golden, above the Columbia confluence
spots.season: Aug-Oct after glacial melt settles; kokanee run in fall
spots.access: Blaeberry Valley roads off Hwy 1 NW of Golden; mix of road access and short walks. Bull trout release in streams.
spots.notes: Prettier and clearer than the Kicking Horse (which stays glacial milk most of the season). Modest fish, great setting. My line is approximate - redraw to your reaches. Seeded ratings are reputation-based.

spots.name: Mitten Lake
spots.detail: Rec site lake in the hills SW of Parson / south of Golden
spots.season: Ice-off through fall; troll deep with baitfish imitations for the big ones
spots.access: Forest roads from Hwy 95 near Parson. Rec site with 27 campsites, dock, small beach, boat launch.
spots.notes: Stocked annually with ~8,000 Gerrard rainbows - the strain that grows into lunkers. The sleeper pick on the Golden end of the valley. Seeded ratings are reputation-based.
